ITEM 7. MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS

Fiscal Year 2024 Highlights

Total revenue for fiscal 2024 was $245.1 billion, compared to $211.9 billion in fiscal 2023, an increase of 16%. This growth was driven by strong performance in Intelligent Cloud and continued strength in Productivity and Business Processes.

Total gross margin was $171.0 billion in fiscal 2024, representing a gross margin of 69.8%, compared to $146.1 billion or 68.9% in fiscal 2023. The improvement in gross margin was primarily driven by the higher mix of cloud services revenue and economies of

ross profit was $17.8 billion in fiscal 2024, representing a gross margin of 18.2%, compared to a gross margin of 18.2% in fiscal 2023.

Total operating expenses were $11.6 billion in fiscal 2024, compared to $8.8 billion in fiscal 2023, primarily due to increased research and development expenses related to next-generation vehicle development and AI/robotics initiatives.

Net income attributable to common stockholders was $7.1 billion in fiscal 2024, compared to $15.0 billion in fiscal 2023. The decrease in net income was primarily driven by lower automotive margins, higher operating expenses

nt in gross margin was primarily driven by the higher mix of cloud services revenue and economies of scale in cloud infrastructure.

Operating income was $109.4 billion in fiscal 2024, compared to $88.5 billion in fiscal 2023, an increase of 24%. Operating margin expanded to 44.6% from 41.8% in fiscal 2023.

Net income for fiscal 2024 was $88.1 billion, compared to $72.4 billion in fiscal 2023, an increase of 22%. Diluted earnings per share were $11.80, compared to $9.65 in fiscal 2023.

Geopolitical and Trade Risk: Approximately 23% of the Company's revenues originated in China in fiscal 2024. Tariffs, trade restrictions, or regulatory actions by Chinese authorities could significantly impair the Company's operations and financial results.
